tool_name: Substation Earthing Grid — Ground Resistance, Touch & Step Voltage Safety Check

inputs.name: Soil Resistivity (ρ)
inputs.note: Field measurement per IEEE Std 81 (Wenner method); sandy/dry ≈ 1000 Ω·m; clay wet ≈ 20 Ω·m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Maximum Ground Fault Current (I_f)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Fault Clearing Time (t_f)
inputs.note: Protection relay clearing time; IEEE 80 uses t_f for shock duration

inputs.name: Surface Crushed Rock / Gravel Layer Resistivity (ρ_s)
inputs.note: Dry crushed granite ≈ 3000–10000 Ω·m; wet gravel ≈ 500–2000 Ω·m
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

formulas: Rg_ohm = soil_resistivity_ohm_m * (1/(4*Math.sqrt(Math.PI*grid_area_m2)) + 1/grid_conductor_length_m * (1 + 1/(1 + burial_depth_m*Math.sqrt(20/grid_area_m2))))   // [Ω] Grid Ground Resistance (R_g) — Sverak Formula Reference: IEEE Std 80-2013 Eq.(57) — Sverak simplified formula for grid resistance
GPR_v = fault_current_kA * 1000 * Rg_ohm   // [V] Ground Potential Rise (GPR) Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §16.1 — GPR = I_f × R_g
IB_mA = 1000 * 0.116 / Math.sqrt(fault_duration_s)   // [mA] Body Current Limit — Fibrillation Threshold (I_B) Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §7.4 Eq.(10) — Dalziel formula: I_B = 0.116/√t_s (50 kg body)

Cs = 1 - 0.09*(1 - soil_resistivity_ohm_m/surface_layer_resistivity) / (2*surface_layer_depth_m + 0.09); Rfoot_ohm = 1.5 * Cs * surface_layer_resistivity; E_touch_v = IB_mA/1000 * (1000 + 2*Rfoot_ohm)   // [V] Tolerable Touch Voltage (E_touch) Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §8.3 Eq.(32) — E_touch50 with surface layer reduction factor C_s
E_step_v = IB_mA/1000 * (1000 + 6*Rfoot_ohm)   // [V] Tolerable Step Voltage (E_step) Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §8.3 Eq.(33) — E_step50 with surface layer

D_m = Math.sqrt(grid_area_m2 / Math.round(grid_conductor_length_m / Math.sqrt(grid_area_m2))); Km = 1/(2*Math.PI) * (Math.log(D_m^2/(16*0.005*burial_depth_m)) + Math.log(D_m/(3.84*burial_depth_m))); Ki = 0.644 + 0.148*4; E_mesh_v = soil_resistivity_ohm_m * fault_current_kA * 1000 * Km * Ki / grid_conductor_length_m   // [V] Mesh Voltage Estimate (E_m) — Simplified Reference: IEEE 80-2013 §16.4 Eq.(80) — mesh voltage; conductor diameter assumed 10 mm

V1.error_msg: Mesh voltage exceeds tolerable touch voltage: increase conductor density (reduce mesh spacing), add conductors, or increase burial depth

V2.error_msg: GPR > 5000 V with R_g > 1 Ω: high-voltage hazard zone — telecommunications and fence isolation required per IEEE 80 §17

V3.error_msg: Conductor density < 0.5 m/m²: sparse grid — mesh voltage will be high; add parallel conductors to achieve minimum 1 m/m² density

smart_warnings.message: Mesh voltage within 20% of tolerable limit: marginal safety margin — add equalising conductors or equipotential mats at operator locations
High soil resistivity (> 1000 Ω·m): achieving low grid resistance requires extensive grid or deep-driven rods — consider chemical ground enhancement
R_g > 1 Ω at high fault current: GPR will be dangerously high — verify grid extends beyond substation fence; add remote earth electrodes
Surface layer resistivity < 500 Ω·m: low benefit from surface treatment — increase layer thickness or use higher-resistivity dry crushed stone
Fault clearing time > 1.0 s: tolerable voltages reduce significantly — upgrade protection relay speed or re-evaluate system protection coordination

fmea.effect: Mesh voltage exceeds tolerable touch voltage: increase conductor density (reduce mesh spacing), add conductors, or increase burial depth
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: GPR > 5000 V with R_g > 1 Ω: high-voltage hazard zone — telecommunications and fence isolation required per IEEE 80 §17
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Conductor density < 0.5 m/m²: sparse grid — mesh voltage will be high; add parallel conductors to achieve minimum 1 m/m² density
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Mesh voltage within 20% of tolerable limit: marginal safety margin — add equalising conductors or equipotential mats at operator locations
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: E_mesh_v > E_touch_v * 0.8

fmea.failureMode: High soil resistivity (> 1000 Ω·m): achieving low grid resistance requires extensive grid or deep-driven rods — consider chemical ground enhancement
R_g > 1 Ω at high fault current: GPR will be dangerously high — verify grid extends beyond substation fence; add remote earth electrodes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rg_ohm > 1.0 && fault_current_kA > 10

fmea.failureMode: Surface layer resistivity < 500 Ω·m: low benefit from surface treatment — increase layer thickness or use higher-resistivity dry crushed stone
Fault clearing time > 1.0 s: tolerable voltages reduce significantly — upgrade protection relay speed or re-evaluate system protection coordination
